purpose: Quantifies the fiscal deficit's direct contribution to corporate earnings and reveals the consolidation paradox: deficit reduction mechanically reduces profits
components: Corporate Profits = Investment + Fiscal Deficit + Net Exports + Consumption from Profit Income - Worker Savings
US deficit contribution estimated at $20-40/share of S&P 500 EPS (7-15%)
Fiscal consolidation of 2-3pp GDP improvement directly reduces earnings base
Profits = Investment + Government Deficit + Net Exports - Household Saving + Capitalist Consumption
Current deficit at ~6-7% GDP channels $20-40/share into S&P 500 EPS
Reflexive loop: deficits → earnings → valuations → wealth effect → consumption
Consolidation scenario: deficit from 6.5% to 4.0% → $10-20/share EPS reduction
Godley sectoral balance extension: private sector surplus/deficit implications
Profits = Investment + Government Deficit + Net Exports − Household Saving + Dividends
Government deficit of 6-7% GDP as the dominant profit source
Sectoral balance identity as the macro accounting constraint
Mundell-Fleming open economy extensions for exchange rate and capital flow implications
current_assessment: The fiscal deficit is the load-bearing variable sustaining corporate profits at 1.5-2.4pp GDP contribution. Private-sector-generated profits are only sufficient for 1.0-1.5% growth. All 8 agents converge on this framework, which is itself flagged as a risk signal — unanimity on an unfalsifiable framework may indicate analytical monoculture.
limitations: Does not account for second-order effects (monetary easing response, exchange rate adjustment)
Framework is static — does not model dynamic adjustment paths
equity_analyst_02 entirely missed this channel despite being an earnings specialist
Cannot predict political probability of fiscal consolidation
Speed and sequencing of consolidation affects transmission
Reflexive loop dynamics are hard to model precisely
DOGE and other fiscal reform initiatives introduce novel uncertainty
Accounting identity tells you the what but not the when
Framework unanimity across all agents is itself a risk signal
Functionally unfalsifiable — explains why indicators failed, why the cycle extended, why the private sector is healthy
Only one proposed falsification condition: economy grows >2.5% for 4 quarters while deficit narrows >1pp GDP
Ricardian equivalence partial offset (30-50%) is acknowledged by only one agent
Distributional adequacy of the surplus is unaddressed by the aggregate identity
